title: Index out of bounds in regular expression search.
description:
This bug looks like this (closed) bug:
https://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=9288
I have this code (because of a question on stackoverflow.com):

set @q="'в работе','на списание','списано'";
WITH RECURSIVE cte as (
   select 1 as a union all
   select a+1 from cte where a<35
   )
select distinct  regexp_substr(@q,'''[^,]*''',a) as E from cte;

When setting higher values for the number where U currently entered '35' and error "ERROR 3686 (HY000): Index out of bounds in regular expression search."
is returned.
How to repeat:
When doing a single line like this:
select regexp_substr("a,b,c",'''[^,]*''',100) as E ;

The error is also shown.
Suggested fix:
Do not throw this error ...
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value DECIMAL(10,2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test1', 10.50), ('test2', NULL), ('test3', 20.00);
SELECT regexp_matches(name, E'([^,]*)', 'g') AS extracted FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (SELECT 1 AS a UNION ALL SELECT a + 1 FROM cte WHERE a < 100) SELECT DISTINCT regexp_matches('example1,example2,example3', E'([^,]*)', 'g') AS result FROM cte;
key_bug_features: Index out of bounds in regexp_substr function
